item.title: Euro-Zone Economy Shrank at Start of Year Because of Ireland
item.description: The euro-zone economy shrank at the start of the year after an unprecedented contraction in Ireland forced a revision to data that originally showed feeble growth.

item.title: Anthropic Calls for AI Pause Button to Let Humans Take Stock
item.description: Anthropic PBC called for the creation of an industrywide mechanism to pause the development of artificial intelligence, giving society the opportunity to “deal with its immense implications.”

item.title: Telenor Is Said to Explore Sale of Digital Bank in Pakistan
item.description: Telenor ASA is considering selling its controlling stake in its digital bank in Pakistan, people familiar with the matter said, a move that would mark its full exit from the South Asian nation.

item.title: BOE Survey Shows Firms Plan Price Hikes to Protect Margins
item.description: UK firms expect to raise prices by 4% in the coming year, according to a Bank of England survey that suggests companies are trying to pass on higher energy costs to consumers.

item.title: Taiwan Inflation Tops Central Bank Alert Level on Oil Prices
item.description: Taiwan’s consumer inflation sped past the monetary authority’s alert level, strengthening the case for further rate increases.

item.title: Carmignac Sees Deficits Making Central Banks Inflation Tolerant
item.description: Carmignac is extending the maturity of its inflation-linked bond holdings in a bet that mounting pressure on government budgets will push central banks to tolerate higher inflation in coming years.

item.title: Danantara Plans Low-Yield Bond as Investors Shun Indonesia
item.description: Indonesia’s sovereign wealth fund Danantara is planning to sell local-currency bonds that will pay interest below market rates at a time when global investors are rapidly losing confidence in the nation’s markets.

item.title: Inside Hudson River Trading's Blistering Token Burn
item.description: How the market-maker is spending on AI.

item.title: Global Food Prices Steady Near Highest Level in Three Years
item.description: Global food prices remained near the highest level in more than three years, after a drop in the cost of palm and soy oils countered disruptions to key agricultural input flows from the Iran war.

item.title: Odd Lots: Inside Hudson River Trading’s Token Burn (Podcast)
item.description: Today’s episode, which was recorded at our recent live show at New York’s City Winery, follows up on a conversation we had with Iain Dunning, head of AI at Hudson River Trading. Last year, we talked about how his firm uses AI. Now, some seven months later, we follow up on how one of the biggest market makers around is deploying this technology. We talk about the price of memory, bottlenecks in compute, how much HRT employees are actually spending on tokens, why the firm might develop its own chi

item.title: ECB to Hike Twice With Inflation Above Comfort Zone, Poll Shows
item.description: The European Central Bank will respond to the Iran war by raising interest rates twice this year before them keeping steady for longer than previously thought, economists say.

item.title: Hon Hai Sales Growth Accelerates Beyond Estimates in May
item.description: Hon Hai Precision Industry Co.’s revenue rose 34% in the April to May period, underscoring demand for the Nvidia Corp. servers essential to AI infrastructure.

item.title: China Allows Some Banks to Offer Higher Rates on Dollar Deposits
item.description: China is allowing some banks to offer higher interest rates on corporate US dollar deposits, according to people familiar with the matter, a move that may damp the yuan’s recent gains by encouraging companies to slow their currency conversion.

item.title: Jollibee Is Said to Mull Moving US Listing to Booming Hong Kong
item.description: Jollibee Foods Corp. is considering switching a potential spinoff and listing of its international business from the US to Hong Kong, where share sales are flying, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Purbaya Slams ‘Sell Indonesia’ Narrative, Points to Bond Inflows
item.description: Indonesian Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa defended his country’s economic track record amid a slump in the rupiah and stocks, pointing to inflows into the debt market as proof investors remain optimistic.

item.title: Once a Turkey Bull, Algebris Shifts to CDS as Credit Risks Rise
item.description: Algebris Investments is buying default protection on Turkish bonds, as it sees a higher probability of a credit event with the Iran war increasing strains on the economy.

item.title: EM Stocks, Currencies Dragged Down by Korean Market Selloff
item.description: Emerging market assets fell for the third straight session, led by a selloff in South Korean equities as investor enthusiasm for artificial intelligence stocks waned.

item.title: China Crude Buying Seen Languishing for Months as Demand Tumbles
item.description: China’s crude imports plunged in May to the lowest level in a decade and could languish for the coming months, as weaker demand, refinery cuts and limited exports help the world’s top buyer weather the impact of the Iran War.

item.title: Ghana Central Bank Head Sees Scope for Rate Cuts After Iran War
item.description: Bank of Ghana Governor Johnson Asiama played down the weakness of the cedi this year and said policymakers could resume cutting interest rates once the Iran war ends.

item.title: Turkish Annual Inflation Ticks Higher but Monthly Pressures Ease
item.description: Turkish inflation accelerated for a second consecutive month in May, though a slowdown in monthly price growth may ease pressure on policymakers ahead of an interest-rate decision next week.

item.title: Kazakhstan Surprises With First Cut to Interest Rate Since 2024
item.description: Kazakhstan unexpectedly cut its key interest rate for the first time in almost two years as inflation in the Central Asian oil producer continued to slow, but signaled policy would remain relatively tight.

item.title: US Firm to Build $6.2 Billion Lesotho Hydro Project, Data Center
item.description: Convalt Energy, a US-based company, agreed to spend $6.2 billion to build a 1,200-megawatt hydro-power project and an artificial intelligence data center in Lesotho.

item.title: European Gas Set for Weekly Gain With US-Iran Deal Still Elusive
item.description: European natural gas prices headed for a weekly gain as uncertainty over the prospects for a peace deal between the US and Iran dominated sentiment after fighting in the Middle East intensified.

item.title: Chinese Firm Seeks $2.6 Billion Loan for Hong Kong Data Center
item.description: Chinese data center operator Range Intelligent Computing Technology Group Co. is seeking a loan of around HK$20 billion ($2.6 billion) to fund what would be Hong Kong’s largest computing facility, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Japan Spent Billions to Prop Up the Yen. Why Is It Still Weak?
item.description: The yen’s weakness has become a growing issue for Japan’s policymakers, given its impact on import prices and household costs.

item.title: B&M Ignites Short Squeeze as Stock Heads For Best Ever Week
item.description: A record rally in B&M European Value Retail Plc has squeezed short sellers betting against the stock, after the UK discounter’s better-than-expected earnings bolstered confidence in its turnaround effort.

item.title: UK’s Biggest Retailers Cut 18,000 Jobs Since Labour’s Tax Rises
item.description: Britain’s largest retailers have shed almost 18,000 jobs in the past year, as the industry grapples with higher taxes and wages since Labour came to power.

item.title: Southeast Asia Enjoys Some Inflation Relief as Oil Prices Ease
item.description: Inflation has decelerated in the Philippines and Thailand as global crude costs eased, giving their central banks more breathing room as they assess the need to further raise interest rates.

item.title: Korean Police Launch Gambling Probe Targeting Polymarket Users
item.description: South Korean police have launched an investigation into local users of Polymarket over alleged illegal gambling, as the prediction-market platform faces mounting regulatory scrutiny while expanding its presence in Asia.

item.title: FTSE 100 Outperforms Thanks to Low Tech Exposure
